repo.name: commander.js
repo.description: The complete solution for node.js command-line interfaces.

sources.notes: README positioning, install command, quickstart, options, commands, automated help, TypeScript, and examples references.
Package description, keywords, license, module exports, TypeScript types, scripts, and Node engine.
README links to split.js, string-util.js, and other examples.

facts.value: The complete solution for node.js command-line interfaces.
Commander lets developers describe a command line interface, parses arguments into options and command arguments, displays usage errors, and implements help.
Commander supports boolean options, options with values, default values, required options, variadic options, version options, and custom option processing.
Commander supports commands, command arguments, action handlers, stand-alone executable subcommands, and life cycle hooks.
Commander implements automated help with custom help, error help display, command help, help groups, and description/summary support.
package.json exports CommonJS, ESM, and TypeScript type definitions.

product.name: Commander.js
product.oneLiner: The complete solution for Node.js command-line interfaces.
problems: Command-line programs need reliable parsing for options, arguments, usage errors, and help output.
Small CLIs and multi-command tools need the same primitives without building parsers from scratch.
features: Boolean and value options with defaults, required values, and custom processing
Commands, command arguments, action handlers, and subcommands
Automated help output with custom help configuration
CommonJS, ESM, and TypeScript type entrypoints
useCases: Build a small Node.js executable with options and arguments.
Create a multi-command CLI with per-command help.
Share one parser setup across JavaScript and TypeScript projects.
